what_it_does: A deterministic, model-free, network-free Python extractor that parses Claude-Code session transcripts (*.jsonl) into EXCHANGE units — each one Tim-message paired with the assistant's joined text reply. These units feed capture(role='mine_exchange', projection='history') for the ③ transcript-miner. It caps text per exchange, exchanges per file, and selects files by size, batching incrementally toward the full corpus.
observations: Module docstring identifies it as ③ the transcript-miner's deterministic EXTRACT (G23), promoted from proven mine-lane logic; emphasizes 'no model, no network', pure extraction is the floor
Constants: MAX_TIM=1200, MAX_REPLY=2000, MIN_TIM=25 (a Tim message shorter than 25 chars is treated as a non-exchange ack/handle)
_text_of handles both string content and content-block lists, joining only type=='text' blocks and skipping tool blocks
_is_tool_result_record encodes the 'reply-boundary fix': a tool_result rides as type='user' and must NOT start a new exchange or truncate the reply
extract_exchanges streams the file line-by-line, resilient to bad JSON lines (continue on JSONDecodeError/UnicodeDecodeError), and to a missing file (returns [])
Exchange-close logic: a real Tim message closes the previous {session,ts,tim,reply} unit; caps reply to MAX_REPLY; honors max_exchanges (default 40); flushes a trailing exchange at EOF

transcripts_by_size selects .jsonl files >= min_kb (default 30KB) and orders mid-size first, deprioritizing huge files (>max_mb=20MB) via a sort-key trick (adds 2*max + sz so they sort last but still get mined later)
Units carry session (basename before first dot), timestamp, capped tim text, and joined reply
Notes the history space is the ⑨ durable-memory substrate (corpus, NOT episodic-memory — Tim's standing rule)
standouts: The reply-boundary fix: recognizing that tool_result records are type='user' and excluding them so a tool round-trip doesn't falsely split or truncate an exchange
The deliberately deterministic floor — extraction carries no model and no network, leaving judgment to downstream roles
The size-sort trick that mines mid-size dialogue-rich transcripts first while still eventually covering huge tool-noisy ones via the per-file cap
conventions: Follows the incremental-batch shape explicitly likened to exocortex_ingest (caps + batching toward the full set)
Resilient/fail-soft parsing at the line level (skip bad lines) rather than aborting a file
Pure-function style with keyword-only caps and small private helpers; docstrings explain intent and the standing rules behind choices
concerns: Head+tail truncation is via simple slicing ([:MAX_REPLY] / [:MAX_TIM]) — the docstring says 'head + tail carry the substance' but the code keeps only the head, so the tail is dropped despite the comment
An assistant reply arriving before any qualifying Tim message (tim_msg is None) is silently discarded, and sub-MIN_TIM Tim messages drop their following reply entirely
notes: A small deterministic transcript→exchange extractor (the floor of the ③ miner) whose key subtlety is treating tool_result records as non-Tim so exchanges aren't wrongly split.
questions: Is the head-only truncation intentional given the docstring claims head+tail are kept?
Where is the calling driver that batches transcripts_by_size into capture(role='mine_exchange')?
How are the timestamp and session keys used downstream (e.g. for the temporal axis / exchange:// idempotency keys mentioned elsewhere)?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches: it is the deterministic extract feeding the miner exactly as stated; the only divergence is the docstring's 'head + tail' claim versus the code's head-only slicing.
apparent_themes: deterministic floor — keep model judgment out of extraction
exchange (Tim-turn + reply) as the unit of mining
resilient incremental batching toward a full corpus
transcripts as the durable-memory (history space) substrate, not episodic memory
intention_signals: Trying to turn raw session transcripts into clean, capped, dialogue-focused exchange units that downstream model-driven mining can judge, while keeping the extraction step pure, deterministic, and robust to malformed lines and tool-result noise.
novelty: The explicit tool_result-as-type='user' reply-boundary handling and the size-sort trick that prioritizes mid-size dialogue-rich transcripts while still queuing huge ones.
contribution: Contributes the deterministic extract primitive — the exact exchange-unit shape {session,ts,tim,reply}, the reply-boundary rule, and the size-prioritized batching — that a fused system would keep as the model-free front of the transcript-mining pipeline.
summary_for_embedding: A deterministic model-free Python extractor that parses Claude-Code .jsonl transcripts into capped Tim-message/assistant-reply exchange units (handling tool_result boundary records) for the ③ transcript-miner.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the pure deterministic extraction floor that converts session transcripts into clean exchange units for downstream mining into the durable history space.
